description:
You are a technical writer who maintains the versioning and documentation repository located at
    /home/user/project-docs.

    Current repository state:
    1) /home/user/project-docs/VERSION  
       • Single-line file that presently contains exactly “1.4.2” (no extra spaces, one trailing newline).

    2) /home/user/project-docs/CHANGELOG.md  
       • Markdown file that follows “Keep a Changelog” style with this exact content (including blank lines):

            # Changelog

            All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file following Keep a Changelog principles.

            ## [1.4.2] - 2024-01-10
            ### Changed
            - Tweaked API docs.

       • Note the two blank lines after the “# Changelog” heading and one blank line between every section.

    Goal  
    Perform a semantic MINOR version bump from 1.4.2 → 1.5.0 and leave behind a repeatable workflow so that subsequent bumps are automated.

    Required end-state (automated tests will compare byte-for-byte):

    A) /home/user/project-docs/VERSION must now contain  
       1.5.0  
       (and nothing else, still ending with exactly one newline).

    B) /home/user/project-docs/CHANGELOG.md must be rewritten so the new entry is placed directly above the old 1.4.2 entry.  
       The file content must be exactly:

            # Changelog

            All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file following Keep a Changelog principles.

            ## [1.5.0] - 2024-03-25
            ### Added
            - Initial user guides for v1.5 minor release.

            ## [1.4.2] - 2024-01-10
            ### Changed
            - Tweaked API docs.

    C) Create the directory /home/user/project-docs/tools (if it does not exist) and place an executable Bash script named bump_version.sh there.  
       Functional requirements for bump_version.sh:
       • Syntax:  ./bump_version.sh <major|minor|patch>  
       • It must read the current numeric version in /home/user/project-docs/VERSION, increment the component specified by the positional argument, and write the new value back to the same file while zeroing the lesser-order components as per semantic-versioning rules (e.g., “1.4.2” minor → “1.5.0”).  
       • It must print the old and new versions to STDOUT exactly in the form “OLD_VERSION→NEW_VERSION” (e.g., “1.4.2→1.5.0”).

       Tests will invoke:  
       /home/user/project-docs/tools/bump_version.sh patch  
       after your work is done.  
       With the repository in its final state (VERSION = 1.5.0) the script must update the file to 1.5.1 and echo “1.5.0→1.5.1”.

    D) Create the directory /home/user/project-docs/.logs and write (overwrite if it already exists) a file named bump_history.log containing exactly one line:

        2024-03-25 10:15:00 - Bumped from 1.4.2 to 1.5.0

       • Keep the newline at the end of the line.  
       • Do not add any other lines.

    Summary of what you have to achieve:  
    1. Write the bump_version.sh script (meeting all functional specs and being executable).  
    2. Use the script (or manual edits) to bring VERSION to 1.5.0.  
    3. Insert the new 1.5.0 section at the top of CHANGELOG.md with the exact Markdown specified.  
    4. Produce the single-line /home/user/project-docs/.logs/bump_history.log documenting this operation.

    The automated grading system will verify:
    • Exact byte-for-byte file contents for VERSION, CHANGELOG.md and bump_history.log.  
    • Presence and executability of /home/user/project-docs/tools/bump_version.sh.  
    • Correct functional output of bump_version.sh when called with “patch” after your modifications.

    Work only inside your home directory; you do not need root privileges.  
    Do not leave any temporary or stray files outside /home/user/project-docs.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

    /home/user/project-docs/        (exists)
    ├── VERSION                      → contains: "1.4.2\n"
    └── CHANGELOG.md                 → contains exactly:
        "# Changelog\n\nAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file following Keep a Changelog principles.\n\n## [1.4.2] - 2024-01-10\n### Changed\n- Tweaked API docs.\n"

    Directories /home/user/project-docs/tools and /home/user/project-docs/.logs do NOT exist at start.

    Expected final state:

    1) /home/user/project-docs/VERSION
       Content: "1.5.0\n"

    2) /home/user/project-docs/CHANGELOG.md
       Content (including all exact line breaks):
       "# Changelog\n\nAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file following Keep a Changelog principles.\n\n## [1.5.0] - 2024-03-25\n### Added\n- Initial user guides for v1.5 minor release.\n\n## [1.4.2] - 2024-01-10\n### Changed\n- Tweaked API docs.\n"

    3) /home/user/project-docs/tools/bump_version.sh
       • Any implementation is acceptable, but the test harness will:
         a) Check the script’s executable bit.  
         b) Run “/home/user/project-docs/tools/bump_version.sh patch” in a clean subshell.  
         c) Expect STDOUT to be exactly: "1.5.0→1.5.1\n"  
            (no extra spaces).  
         d) Then read /home/user/project-docs/VERSION and expect "1.5.1\n".

    4) /home/user/project-docs/.logs/bump_history.log
       Content: "2024-03-25 10:15:00 - Bumped from 1.4.2 to 1.5.0\n"

    File permissions: all files should be user-writable and readable; bump_version.sh must have user execute permission (chmod 755 or 700 is fine).
